message:
fix(tests): join spawned server threads to prevent resource leaks in security_http_policy tests

Five test helper threads were spawned with `let _server = std::thread::spawn(...)`,
discarding the JoinHandle. This means:
- Thread panics would be silently swallowed instead of propagating test failures
- OS thread resources could leak if the test runner exits before threads complete
- Miri/sanitizer runs flag detached threads as potential resource leaks

Changes across 5 tests (response_body_exceeding_limit_returns_error,
request_timeout_returns_timeout_error_code, call_level_timeout_used_when_request_omits_it,
denied_host_never_opens_connection, zero_timeout_treated_as_no_timeout):
- Bind JoinHandle to `server` instead of `_server`
- Call `server.join().unwrap()` at end of each test to ensure clean thread shutdown
  and surface any panics that occurred in the server thread

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
